articles.title: Insurance Broker Howden Inks 32K-SF Deal at Durst’s 151 West 42nd Street
articles.narrative: Global insurance broker Howden has signed a new lease for 31,519 square feet, encompassing the entire 53rd floor, at The Durst Organization's One Five One office tower in Times Square. The building's sustainable design, state-of-the-art amenities, and proximity to Bryant Park were key factors in attracting the tenant, as the firm sought a premium workspace in the heart of New York's financial markets.

data_points.address: 151 West 42nd Street, New York City, NY
data_points.notable_features: Sustainable design, state-of-the-art amenities, and close proximity to Bryant Park, Port Authority Bus Terminal, and Grand Central Terminal.

articles.market_intelligence: Midtown office towers had an average asking rent of $59.62 in the first quarter of 2026.

articles.title: LGT Capital Partners Takes 42K SF at 1251 Avenue of the Americas
articles.narrative: LGT Capital Partners, a global alternative investment specialist, has signed a 10-year lease for 41,600 square feet of office space at 1251 Avenue of the Americas in Midtown Manhattan. The firm is relocating its New York City office from 1133 Avenue of the Americas to this 54-story tower owned by Mitsui Fudosan America. This transaction was one of the largest office leases in Midtown during April, reflecting a strong leasing velocity in Manhattan.

data_points.address: 1251 Avenue of the Americas, New York City, NY
data_points.notable_features: 54-story office tower located between West 49th and West 50th streets.
data_points.project_notes: LGT Capital Partners is relocating from 1133 Avenue of the Americas.

articles.market_intelligence: Midtown office asking rents averaged $84.77 per square foot in April. Manhattan's office leasing velocity reached 3.61 million square feet in April, exceeding the 10-year monthly average of 2.78 million square feet and surpassing April 2025's activity by 6.9 percent.

articles.title: Catalfumo Lands $401M to Finish Ritz-Carlton Condos in Palm Beach Gardens
articles.narrative: Catalfumo Companies has secured a $401 million bridge loan from Northwind to complete its Ritz-Carlton Residences condominium project in Palm Beach Gardens, Florida. The financing will refinance a previous $340 million construction loan and carry the project through its sellout phase. The development, which is already 35% presold, is expected to benefit from strong demand in South Florida.

data_points.property_name: The Ritz-Carlton Residences, Palm Beach Gardens
data_points.address: 2200 PGA Boulevard, Palm Beach Gardens, FL
data_points.notable_features: Waterfront property with 1,040 feet of Intracoastal Waterway frontage, private 29-boat slip marina, 20,000 square feet of amenities including a waterfront restaurant, pool, spa, wellness facilities, fitness center, pickleball court, and clubhouse. Units are three- and four-bedroom floor-through residences.
data_points.project_notes: The loan refinances a $340 million construction loan issued three years prior. Prices range from $4 million to over $10 million. 35% of units are presold.

articles.market_intelligence: South Florida is experiencing sustained migration from the Northeast and an expanding institutional presence, supporting demand for luxury residential projects.

articles.title: Brooklyn Heights Walgreens to Become High-Rise After $30M Sale
articles.narrative: Rogers Equities has acquired a 6,000-square-foot development site at 120 Court Street in Brooklyn Heights, NY, from the Sorkin family for $30 million. The site, currently home to a Walgreens pharmacy, is slated for a 14-story, 75-unit mixed-use residential building with ground-floor retail. The transaction was facilitated by Newmark, representing both the buyer and seller, and marks a significant development in the upscale Brooklyn Heights neighborhood.

data_points.notable_features: High-profile corner location at Atlantic Avenue and Court Street, directly across from a Trader Joe's, with 75 feet of frontage on Atlantic Avenue and 80 feet on Court Street. Plans include recreational space, 38-bike storage, and amenity spaces on the first and second floors with outdoor recreation on the roof.
data_points.project_notes: The site is currently occupied by a Walgreens pharmacy, which will close in June 2026. The new development will be a high-end rental building under New York State's 485x property tax incentive program. Plans were filed in March 2026.

articles.title: Continental Realty Acquires 14-Property U.S. Shopping Center Portfolio
articles.narrative: Continental Realty has acquired a portfolio of 14 shopping centers totaling over 2 million square feet across seven states in the Southeast and Midwest from U.S. Properties Group for approximately $200 million. This off-market transaction represents one of the largest U.S. retail portfolio deals this year, expanding Continental Realty's national shopping center portfolio to over 10.5 million square feet. The acquisition aligns with Continental Realty's strategy to invest in necessity-based retail in secondary markets, driven by population growth and affordability trends.

data_points.notable_features: Portfolio includes necessity-based retail centers with strong tenant mixes including major retailers.
data_points.project_notes: The portfolio spans Georgia, Illinois, Tennessee, Ohio, North Carolina, South Carolina, and Virginia. The largest center in the portfolio is the 300,000-square-foot Streets of Indian Lake in Nashville, TN. This acquisition marks Continental Realty's first entry into Ohio with four shopping centers totaling 435,000 square feet.

articles.market_intelligence: Retail occupancy in the secondary markets covered by the portfolio averages 97 percent. CoStar Group data indicates this acquisition is among the largest U.S. retail portfolio transactions of the year so far.

articles.title: Town Lane, Gillon Property Acquire 460,000 SF Retail, Office Property in Allen, Texas
articles.narrative: A partnership between Town Lane and Gillon Property Group has acquired Watters Creek Village, a 460,000-square-foot retail and office property in Allen, Texas. The property, built in 2008 on 46 acres, includes a significant retail component anchored by a grocery store and boutique office space. The new owners plan to implement capital improvements.

data_points.notable_features: Anchored by a Market Street grocery store; includes 360,000 square feet of retail and 100,000 square feet of boutique office space.

articles.title: JLL Brokers Sale of 271,689 SF Industrial Campus in Cedar Park, Texas
articles.narrative: JLL has brokered the sale of New Hope, a newly constructed three-building industrial campus totaling 271,689 square feet in Cedar Park, Texas. The campus was sold by a joint venture of Central Texas investment firms to San Francisco-based Stockbridge. The property features modern shallow-bay, rear-load buildings with high clear heights and ample loading and parking.

data_points.address: 200 W New Hope Dr, Cedar Park, TX
data_points.notable_features: Three shallow-bay, rear-load buildings with 32-foot clear heights, 200-foot truck court depths, 17 percent office finishes, 82 dock-high doors, and 579 parking spaces.

articles.title: Hunter Advisors Arranges Sale of 136-Room Hotel in Irving
articles.narrative: Hunter Advisors has arranged the sale of the 136-room Aloft Hotel Las Colinas in Irving, Texas. The buyer, whose identity was not disclosed, plans to implement capital improvements to the property, which was originally built in 2008.

data_points.notable_features: Amenities include an outdoor pool, fitness center, business center, meeting space, and an onsite bar and restaurant.

articles.title: Finial Group Negotiates 12,500 SF Industrial Lease in Northwest Houston
articles.narrative: Finial Group has negotiated a 12,500-square-foot industrial lease in northwest Houston on behalf of Firetrol Protection Systems. The transaction involves the entire building located at 6770 Bourgeois Road.

data_points.project_notes: Building was renovated in 2024.

articles.title: Newmark Negotiates $64M Sale of Apartment Community in Huntsville
articles.narrative: Newmark has brokered the $64 million sale of The Liam at Hays Farm, a 329-unit apartment community in Huntsville, AL. Greystone acquired the property from a joint venture between Bomasada Group and an affiliate of The Wolff Co. This transaction represents the largest multifamily deal in the Huntsville market since 2023.

data_points.notable_features: Located within the 850-acre Hays Farm master-planned community; features a resort-style swimming pool, Zen courtyard with a fireside lounge, and a modern fitness center.

articles.title: Flyover No More: The Case for Midwest Hotel Investment
articles.narrative: This article argues that the Midwest presents a compelling, often overlooked, investment opportunity for hospitality properties, contrasting it with the traditionally favored Sun Belt. The author highlights several advantages, including a balanced demand across cycles, lower supply risk due to limited new hotel construction, and significantly reduced insurance exposure compared to coastal markets. Key demand drivers in the Midwest include diversified economies in cities like Madison, expanding athletic calendars in Big Ten university towns, and robust medical travel anchored by major university hospital systems. While acknowledging seasonality as a downside, the article suggests that strategic investments in these demand-rich areas offer a long-term hedge for investors seeking stability and assets priced below replacement cost.
articles.market_intelligence: Midwest hospitality assets tend to perform on different rhythms than leisure-driven Sun Belt markets. New hotel supply has been limited in Midwest cities like Indianapolis, Chicago, and Milwaukee. Insurance exposure is dramatically lower in most Midwest states compared to coastal and hurricane-prone Sun Belt markets. Medical and hospital-adjacent travel is one of the fastest-growing sources of hotel demand nationally.

articles.title: Vacancy rates still too high in Indianapolis office market. But is stability on the horizon?
articles.narrative: The Indianapolis office market is showing signs of stabilization despite high vacancy rates, according to Colliers' Q1 2026 report. While the overall vacancy rate held steady at 21.2%, the downtown area continues to struggle with a record-high 27.7% vacancy. In contrast, suburban submarkets, particularly those north of the city like Carmel, Fishers, and Keystone Crossing, are experiencing stronger demand for Class-A space, with asking rents in some areas now surpassing the CBD due to a flight-to-quality trend. Leasing activity has increased, with 25,600 square feet of positive net absorption in Q1 and over 2.4 million square feet leased in the past year, a 24% year-over-year increase. Companies are generally reducing their footprints by 25-30% compared to pre-pandemic levels, and over 500,000 square feet of office space has been removed from inventory through conversions, with another 400,000 square feet expected to follow, helping to mitigate rising vacancies.
articles.market_intelligence: Asking rents for top-tier buildings in some suburban submarkets now exceed those in the CBD. Sublease availability has fallen from its peak in 2023. New construction is largely concentrated in suburban, mixed-use environments. Investors are targeting value-add opportunities, particularly in the Northwest submarket, acquiring underperforming assets at reduced prices for repositioning.

articles.title: Marcus & Millichap closes sale of 20-townhome portfolio in New Richmond
articles.narrative: A 20-townhome portfolio in New Richmond, Wisconsin, has been sold for $4.6 million. The transaction was brokered by Marcus & Millichap, representing a Wisconsin-based investment group as the seller and procuring a Wisconsin-based syndication as the buyer. The portfolio consists of three-bedroom, two-bathroom homes built in 2016, located approximately 40 miles from Minneapolis–Saint Paul.

data_points.notable_features: All units are three-bedroom, two-bathroom homes.

articles.title: CRC closes acquisition of 14-property retail portfolio spanning seven states
articles.narrative: Continental Realty Corporation (CRC) has completed an off-market acquisition of a 14-property retail portfolio from US Properties Group. The portfolio, spanning over 2 million square feet across seven states, includes a mix of well-located retail assets with strong market fundamentals and high occupancy. This strategic acquisition expands CRC's national shopping center portfolio and increases its assets under management to nearly $5 billion, broadening its footprint to 16 states.

data_points.notable_features: Portfolio includes a lifestyle center, Streets of Indian Lake, in Nashville, TN, and four assets in Ohio, including the Kroger-anchored Delaware Community Plaza.
data_points.project_notes: This off-market transaction is the third largest portfolio acquisition of the year according to CoStar Group.

articles.market_intelligence: The acquired portfolio is located in markets with an average retail occupancy of 97 percent. Delaware County, part of the Columbus metropolitan area, experienced 12.54% population growth over the past five years, and the Columbus market added over 21,000 residents in 2025.

articles.title: Lee & Associates negotiates 10,773-square-foot industrial lease in Fort Worth
articles.narrative: Lee & Associates Dallas-Fort Worth has brokered a new industrial lease for a 10,773-square-foot space at Northern Cross-Building D in Fort Worth, Texas. Premier Medical Distribution will occupy the space, with Lee & Associates representing the tenant and Holt Lunsford Commercial representing the landlord, ULF Northern Crossing LP.

data_points.address: 3051 Northern Cross Blvd., Fort Worth, TX

articles.title: World’s largest bicycle manufacturer rides into Boulder
articles.narrative: Giant Group U.S.A., the American subsidiary of the world's largest bicycle manufacturer, has signed a 10-year lease for 44,000 square feet at 3825 Walnut Street in Boulder, CO, establishing its new U.S. headquarters. The move from Southern California to Boulder, a prominent cycling hub, will consolidate operations and includes plans for a nine-month renovation to create retail, showroom, and office space within the largely warehouse building. The relocation is expected to bring approximately 125 jobs to the area.

data_points.notable_features: Building is almost entirely a warehouse with roughly 16,000 square feet of office space; will include retail space, a showroom, and office space.
data_points.project_notes: The building owner previously planned to redevelop the property into a life sciences campus. Giant Group U.S.A. is moving from Newbury Park, CA, and plans to move next year following a nine-month renovation. The relocation deepens Giant's foothold in Boulder after its subsidiary SPIA Cycling acquired Stages Cycling out of bankruptcy in 2024; Stages Cycling is expected to vacate its headquarters at 5335 Sterling Drive and fold operations into Giant's new base.

articles.title: Boston-area university sells half of its campus for luxury development
articles.narrative: Salem State University is selling 23 acres of its south campus to AvalonBay Communities and Winn Development Company for a 340-unit luxury multifamily project. The sale allows the university to consolidate its operations and avoid over $50 million in deferred maintenance costs. This initial phase, known as Avalon Salem, is part of a larger Forest River Residences project that will eventually include 145 additional units.

data_points.notable_features: Luxury housing development with over 500 parking spaces.
data_points.project_notes: This is the first phase of the broader Forest River Residences project, which will include another 145 units. The sale is contingent on the university completing its campus consolidation by fall 2027.

articles.title: Colliers-branded TIC deals spark state, federal probes
articles.narrative: A series of tenant-in-common (TIC) deals sponsored by Millcreek Commercial Properties and its principal, Kevin Long, are under state and federal investigation following allegations that retirees were sold high-risk medical office building investments under the guise of a global brokerage brand. Investors claim they were misled by inflated valuations and illusory leases to fast-growing healthcare tenants, many of whom later filed for bankruptcy without occupying space or paying rent. The investments, often part of 1031 exchanges, have resulted in significant losses for retirees, leading to at least 14 lawsuits across five states and probes by the SEC and Utah's Division of Real Estate.

articles.title: Starwood faces default on $265M hotel portfolio loan
articles.narrative: A $265 million commercial mortgage-backed securities loan secured by a 22-property hotel portfolio owned by an affiliate of Starwood Capital Group has been transferred to special servicing due to an imminent monetary default. The portfolio, comprising 2,943 keys across 12 states, has seen its financial performance significantly deteriorate, with a debt service coverage ratio of 0.64 and occupancy at 63 percent. Starwood has submitted a modification proposal to the noteholders to address the distress.

data_points.project_notes: Loan originated in 2018 and matures in 2027. The portfolio's debt service coverage ratio plummeted to 0.64 by mid-2025 from 2.07 at origination. Starwood has submitted a modification proposal to noteholders.

articles.title: Global Net Lease acquires industrial REIT for $535M
articles.narrative: Global Net Lease (GNL) has agreed to acquire Modiv Industrial, an industrial REIT, in an all-stock transaction valued at $535 million. The deal, which includes GNL assuming Modiv's debt and preferred stock, is expected to close in the third quarter. This acquisition is part of GNL's strategy to accelerate earnings growth and reduce its office exposure, leveraging Modiv's portfolio of net-leased industrial assets with a weighted average lease term of 15 years and 2.4% annual rent increases.

data_points.notable_features: Modiv's net-leased industrial assets have a weighted average lease term of 15 years with 2.4% annual rent increases.
data_points.project_notes: The deal values Modiv's stock at $18.82 per share, a 17% premium on its closing price on Friday. GNL will pay off Modiv's balance sheet debt and preferred stock. GNL stockholders will own 89% of the combined company, while Modiv stockholders will own 11%.

articles.title: NYC’s top deals: Hedge funder’s estranged wife takes over $30M townhouse amid divorce
articles.narrative: The Archdiocese of New York sold the ground lease for the Lotte New York Palace hotel and an adjacent building for $491.1 million to the Seoul-based hotel operator. This significant transaction was undertaken to fund settlements for survivors of clergy sexual abuse. Additionally, RXR sold a 550,000-square-foot development site in Clinton Hill for $121.4 million to YS Developers, who plan a mixed-use project. A two-story retail property in Brooklyn Heights, occupied by a pharmacy, also traded for $30 million.

data_points.address: 455-457 Madison Avenue, New York City, NY
data_points.project_notes: Sale includes a two-story neighboring building at 35 East 50th Street. Proceeds will fund settlements for survivors of clergy sexual abuse.

companies_people.label: SELLER
companies_people.firm_name: Companies linked to Ken Segal, Maurice Setton and Jeffrey Sorkin

articles.title: PolicyPro: Upstate bill targets $2.5M pied-à-terres; BHS, Corcoran join push against NYC tax
articles.narrative: New York State Senator Patricia Faye has introduced a bill to implement a pied-à-terre tax on second homes in upstate municipalities, targeting properties valued between $2.5 million and $5 million. This threshold is notably lower than Governor Kathy Hochul's proposed $5 million minimum for New York City. The tax rates would range from 0.5% to 4%, with revenue split between local municipalities and the state's Aid and Incentives for Municipalities program. The initiative aims to redirect funds from vacation areas like the Hamptons and Saratoga Springs to surrounding communities in need. However, the proposal faces opposition from real estate professionals and some policy experts who argue it could deter investment and that the upstate second home market may not generate significant revenue.

companies_people.label: OTHER
companies_people.firm_name: Real Estate Board of New York

companies_people.label: OTHER
companies_people.firm_name: NYC Deputy Mayor for Housing and Planning

people.name: Leila Bozorg
people.title: Deputy Mayor for Housing and Planning

articles.title: Vornado exec says they are “ready to roll” on 350 Park
articles.narrative: Vornado Realty Trust is moving forward with its 2 million-square-foot office tower at 350 Park Avenue in New York City, despite speculation that Citadel might withdraw from the project. A Vornado executive confirmed that demolition has begun and the firm is "ready to roll," with a mid-July deadline to commit fully to the joint venture or sell its stake. The executive indicated Vornado's intention to remain involved, noting strong tenant interest years before the building's completion, reflecting a tight market for high-quality office space. The project is a joint venture with Rudin and Citadel.

data_points.address: 350 Park Avenue, New York City, NY
data_points.project_notes: Vornado faces a mid-July deadline to either fully commit to the joint venture or sell its stake. Vornado and Rudin have the option to acquire an interest between 23 percent and 40 percent.

articles.market_intelligence: Class B office buildings are becoming viable for investment, with landlords making renovations and capital improvements. The New York City office market is tight, with tenants calling for presentations on new buildings years in advance.

articles.title: Mamdani housing official calls RGB “very blunt tool”
articles.narrative: A top housing official in the Mamdani administration, Leila Bozorg, acknowledged that New York City's Rent Guidelines Board (RGB) uses a "very blunt tool" by applying uniform rent increases across the diverse rent-stabilized housing stock. While not directly endorsing differentiated rent increases based on building characteristics like age, Bozorg indicated a need for a more holistic approach, including city investment in programs like an insurance initiative for affordable and rent-stabilized buildings, to support owners in covering rising costs. This comes as both the administration and landlord groups are recognizing the financial distress in a growing portion of older rent-stabilized buildings, with landlord associations advocating for split rent orders to address the higher maintenance needs of pre-1974 properties.

companies_people.label: OTHER
companies_people.firm_name: Small Property Owners of New York

articles.title: Cobble Hill’s most expensive listing finds a buyer after a $5M price cut
articles.narrative: A townhouse at 205 Clinton Street in Cobble Hill, Brooklyn, has found a buyer after its asking price was reduced by $5 million from its initial $22 million. The property, which underwent over two years of renovations, was listed at $17 million. If it closes near this price, it is expected to be among the most expensive single-family home sales in the borough this year, potentially surpassing a $16.3 million penthouse sale in Dumbo.

data_points.notable_features: 25-foot-wide home with seven bedrooms, seven bathrooms, an elevator, finished cellar, outdoor kitchen, and a top-floor 'clubhouse' with a terrace. Renovations maintained original features while adding modern amenities.
data_points.project_notes: Property was previously acquired for $3.9 million in 2020. The asking price was initially $22 million in October, cut to $18.5 million in November, and then to $17 million in April.

articles.title: Condo project replacing classic Fort Lauderdale hotel moves ahead
articles.narrative: A 54-unit condominium project, Opus at 701, received site-plan approval from the Fort Lauderdale City Commission. The 13-story development will replace the former Manhattan Tower hotel and an apartment building on a 0.78-acre site along the Intracoastal Waterway. The developer acquired the site last year for nearly $20 million. The project will feature a fitness center, spa, and a rooftop infinity-edge pool, with a sculpture of the former hotel planned for the lobby entrance to commemorate its architectural identity.

data_points.address: 632 and 701 Bayshore Drive, Fort Lauderdale, FL
data_points.notable_features: Boutique condo development with a fitness center, spa, pool deck overlooking the Intracoastal Waterway, and an elevated roof deck with an infinity-edge pool and clubhouse lounge.
data_points.project_notes: The 0.78-acre site was assembled last year for nearly $20 million, including the $11 million purchase of the 1955-built Manhattan Tower hotel and an $8.5 million purchase of a 56-year-old apartment building. A sculpture of the Manhattan Tower will be installed at the lobby entrance to commemorate the property's architectural identity.

articles.title: “90% there”: Controversial project outside UDB delayed again. But is it inching closer?
articles.narrative: A controversial proposal for an industrial facility outside Miami-Dade County's Urban Development Boundary (UDB) has been delayed again, but appears to be inching closer to approval. Kelly Tractor, a heavy machinery supplier, seeks to relocate its headquarters to a 200-acre tract it owns, a move opposed by environmental groups due to its location beyond the UDB. The project, which would create approximately 1,300 jobs, requires a supermajority of nine votes from the county commission to proceed, and the developer is working to secure the final vote by addressing concerns about wetlands preservation and the necessity of building outside the UDB.

data_points.notable_features: Proposed headquarters for a heavy machinery supplier, located outside the Urban Development Boundary.
data_points.project_notes: The project involves preserving 63.1 acres of wetlands on-site and 20 acres of off-site wetlands mitigation. It is seeking approval via a text amendment, which has been a point of contention.

articles.title: “There is no ceiling”: Nadim Ashi’s latest Surf Club condos near $400M sellout
articles.narrative: Fort Partners has achieved a nearly $400 million sellout for its recently completed Seaway North condo building, part of the larger Surf Club complex in Surfside, FL. The 10-unit building, located at 9165 Collins Avenue, boasts an unparalleled average sale price of $38.6 million per unit in Miami-Dade County's new development market. This success is attributed to the collaboration with the Four Seasons brand, high-end finishes, and the boutique nature of the project, which has garnered "it-building" status amid a significant wealth migration to South Florida.

data_points.notable_features: Part of the sprawling beachfront Surf Club complex, with access to Four Seasons amenities, four pools, and a Thomas Keller restaurant. Units feature luxury finishes and a private pool in PH 10.
data_points.project_notes: The Surf Club complex includes the original two-tower Four Seasons Residences (completed 2017), the 26-unit Seaway South (completed 2024), and the planned 17-unit Surf House.

articles.market_intelligence: The South Florida condo market is experiencing high demand for ultraluxury sales, driven by wealth migration to the region. The Surf Club complex is achieving some of the highest prices per square foot in the market, with brokers predicting $6,000 per square foot will soon be the norm within the complex due to limited inventory and strong buyer interest.

articles.title: David MacNeil sells another Fort Lauderdale mansion for $26M
articles.narrative: David MacNeil, founder of WeatherTech, sold a beachfront mansion in Fort Lauderdale, FL for $26 million to Pablo Umansky. This marks the second time MacNeil has sold the property in three years, having previously reacquired it through a foreclosure suit against the prior owner, Jack Owoc, who had financed his initial purchase with a loan from MacNeil. The property features five bedrooms, six bathrooms, four half-bathrooms, a pool, and 100 feet of beachfront.

data_points.address: 3052 North Atlantic Boulevard, Fort Lauderdale, FL
data_points.notable_features: Beachfront property with a pool and 100 feet of beachfront.
data_points.project_notes: Property was previously sold by MacNeil in 2023 for $40 million and reacquired by him in 2024 for $377,800 via a foreclosure suit.

articles.title: Integra sells bayfront Miami-Dade rental complex for $151M
articles.narrative: RPM Living has acquired Biscayne Shores, a 380-unit bayfront apartment and townhome complex in Miami-Dade County, from Integra Investments for $151.4 million. Completed in 2024, the property consists of a 15-story apartment building and three-story townhomes, and was fully leased at the time of sale. This transaction represents one of the largest multifamily trades in Miami-Dade County this year, with brokers noting the rarity of large waterfront apartment developments in the market.

data_points.notable_features: Bayfront property consisting of a 15-story building with 288 apartments and 92 townhomes in three-story buildings.

articles.title: Shorewood scoops up wiped-clean Walgreens campus in Deerfield for $13M
articles.narrative: Shorewood Development Group acquired the former 37.5-acre Walgreens office campus in Deerfield, IL, for $13.1 million from Orion Properties. The site, which was recently cleared of its outdated office buildings, is slated for a future mixed-use development incorporating retail, medical, and multifamily components. This acquisition marks another significant suburban redevelopment for Shorewood, following a trend of transforming large, vacant corporate sites.

data_points.notable_features: Former Walgreens office campus, recently demolished to create a blank slate for redevelopment.
data_points.project_notes: The previous office buildings were demolished by the seller in late 2025 after Walgreens stopped paying rent in August 2023. A prior sale attempt to LG Development Group failed in 2023.

articles.title: Johnson digs in against Bears stadium tax break as Arlington Heights push advances
articles.narrative: Chicago Mayor Brandon Johnson is opposing a proposed property tax break for the Chicago Bears' potential domed stadium in Arlington Heights, arguing it would divert public resources to a wealthy franchise at the expense of city residents and workers. He views the subsidy as a 'giveaway to billionaires' and is leveraging his political influence to block the 'megaprojects bill' in the Illinois Senate. Johnson contends that subsidizing a suburban stadium would undermine economic opportunities for Chicago workers, particularly minority contractors, and reiterated his interest in keeping the team within the city, despite the Bears' focus on suburban and out-of-state options.

articles.title: Al Friedman plots Medinah Temple’s next act as Bally’s prepares to exit
articles.narrative: Albert Friedman's Friedman Properties is seeking a new flagship tenant for the historic 132,600-square-foot Medinah Temple in Chicago's River North, as Bally's temporary casino prepares to vacate the space by spring 2027. Friedman is targeting global brands for an experiential retail concept, moving away from traditional storefronts. This high-profile search is expected to indicate the strength of downtown Chicago's post-pandemic retail recovery.

data_points.notable_features: Historic Moorish Revival building; previously housed Bloomingdale's Home Store and Bally's temporary casino.
data_points.project_notes: Friedman Properties acquired the building in 2019 for approximately $25 million after Macy's closed Bloomingdale's Home Store. Bally's lease, signed in 2022, generated approximately $17 million in rent and management fees over four years and shifted the annual property tax burden of over $1.1 million to Bally's.

articles.title: Lake Forest estate leads slower April for Chicagoland luxury homes
articles.narrative: Chicagoland's luxury residential market experienced a slowdown in April, with only four homes selling for over $5 million, a decrease from seven in March. The top transaction was the $6.3 million sale of a 7,300-square-foot English Country estate in Lake Forest, previously owned by the late Kraft Foods and Quaker Oats CEO Robert Morrison. Other notable sales included an off-market deal for a Lincoln Park home sold by Shannon Abloh for $6.1 million, a $5.5 million home in Winnetka's Hubbard Woods neighborhood, and a $5.1 million duplex at One Magnificent Mile sold by trading card mogul Matthew Bayer.

data_points.address: 745 East Woodland Road, Lake Forest, IL
data_points.notable_features: English Country estate, remodeled between 2022 and 2024, a short walk from Lake Michigan.
data_points.project_notes: Previously acquired in 2020 for $2.9 million.

articles.title: Architect buys Bayou Woods spec mansion asking $11M
articles.narrative: Levant Luxury Homes sold a newly built 12,145-square-foot spec mansion in Houston's exclusive Bayou Woods neighborhood to architect Dan Boggio and Susan Boggio. The property, which was asking $10.5 million, features six bedrooms, 10 bathrooms, a four-car garage, and terraces overlooking a pool, all situated on an acre lot. This sale follows Levant Luxury Homes' previous high-value residential transactions in the area, underscoring the continued demand for luxury homes in this prominent Houston enclave.

data_points.notable_features: Six-bedroom, 10-bathroom home with a four-car garage and upstairs terraces overlooking the pool, located in the upscale Bayou Woods subdivision with 24-hour security and a strong school district.
data_points.project_notes: The home was built in 2025 and was initially listed for $10.5 million in June 2025, relisted in December 2025 after a brief removal.

articles.title: DuWest Realty merges with DBA Commercial Real Estate to form DuWest
articles.narrative: Dallas-based commercial real estate firms DuWest Realty and DBA Commercial Real Estate have merged to form a new entity, simply named DuWest. The combined firm brings together over 50 tenant clients and 3.5 million square feet of managed retail property, with a total of 52 employees and 33 brokers. The merger creates a significant retail-focused brokerage and property management firm in Texas, with offices in Dallas, Houston, Austin, and San Antonio.

articles.title: Nate Paul’s Downtown Austin IHOP sells after bidding war at foreclosure auction
articles.narrative: A downtown Austin IHOP property, previously owned by Nate Paul's World Class Holdings, was sold at a foreclosure auction for approximately $12.7 million to Travis County Exchange Corporation. The 0.8-acre site, located in the Rainey Street District, traded for about half its appraised value, presenting a redevelopment opportunity despite an existing tenant lease extending until at least June 2029. The sale is part of the ongoing disposition of Paul's portfolio following his legal issues.

data_points.address: 707 East Cesar Chavez Street, Austin, TX
data_points.notable_features: Located in Austin's Rainey Street District, near downtown and the Austin Convention Center.
data_points.project_notes: The property was appraised at $26.2 million by the Travis Central Appraisal District. The existing tenant's lease runs until at least June 2029.

articles.title: Foreclosure imminent for Bechtel’s old Houston office
articles.narrative: Lenders are initiating foreclosure proceedings on the 19-story, 441,523-square-foot office tower at 3000 Post Oak Boulevard in Houston, owned by Korea-based AIP Asset Management and Stamford, Connecticut-based Five Mile Capital Partners. The building has faced significant challenges since its anchor tenant, an engineering and construction company, vacated in 2023, leaving the property largely empty. The $80 million loan, originated by JPMorgan Chase & Company in 2020, matured in March 2025 and had been transferred to special servicing twice due to the tenant's departure and the borrowers' payment struggles.

data_points.notable_features: 19-story office tower, last renovated in 2014.
data_points.project_notes: The property was appraised at $143.9 million in 2020 by loan servicers and $45 million by Harris County in 2026. The loan went to special servicing in 2022 and 2024.

articles.market_intelligence: Houston's office market recorded a 27% vacancy rate in Q1 2026, with a significant portion of vacant space concentrated in older, less amenitized buildings.

articles.title: Highland Park Mediterranean-style mansion sells after asking $12M
articles.narrative: A Mediterranean-style mansion in Highland Park, Dallas, was sold by former finance and oil executive Lee Edgar Mikles and his wife Lori Mikles to Michael and Stacey Watson. The property, which last asked $11.5 million, was previously listed for nearly $12 million in September. The buyers secured an $8.1 million loan for the purchase.

data_points.notable_features: Mediterranean-style home with five bedrooms, eight bathrooms, two wet bars, four fireplaces, and a second-story balcony overlooking a walled backyard.
data_points.project_notes: The sellers previously acquired the home in 2023 from Paul Loyd, when it was asking $8 million.

articles.title: Historic office, retail building latest Oakland property to fall into default
articles.narrative: A historic 11,800-square-foot office and retail building in Oakland's Uptown district has defaulted on a $2.4 million loan. The 1923-built property, known as "Radio Central" for its past as a radio station, is owned by an affiliate managed by developer Warren Malnick. This default is part of a broader trend of commercial property distress in Oakland, affecting various asset types including office, multifamily, and hotel properties.

data_points.notable_features: Historic two-story building at the corner of 19th Street and Broadway, designed by Edward Foulkes, and formerly home to radio stations KROW and KABL.

articles.market_intelligence: The Tribune Tower at 409 13th Street, 1540–1544 Broadway, 1500 Broadway, the Financial Center Building, 2101 Webster Street, 1901 Harrison Street, and 2100 Franklin Street are also in default or foreclosure in Oakland. Hotel defaults are particularly increasing across the Bay Area.

articles.title: Santana Row offices reach 100% occupancy with 11K sf PNC Bank lease
articles.narrative: PNC Bank has signed an 11,000-square-foot lease at the One Santana West building, bringing the entire Santana Row mixed-use district's office space to 100% occupancy. This achievement is particularly notable given the elevated office vacancy rates in the broader Silicon Valley market. The developer, Federal Realty Investment Trust, highlighted the strong leasing momentum, which reflects a broader resurgence in the Silicon Valley office market driven by technology and AI tenants.

data_points.notable_features: On-site amenities include a fitness center, golf simulator, and pickleball courts.
data_points.project_notes: The One Santana West building spans 375,000 square feet and is part of the larger Santana Row mixed-use district, which now has approximately 1 million square feet of fully leased office space.

articles.market_intelligence: The Silicon Valley office market recorded a post-pandemic high of 3.2 million square feet in leasing last quarter, a 1.7-million-square-foot increase from the previous quarter. Downtown San Jose had the highest office availability rate last quarter with 36.2 percent, North San Jose at 24.6 percent, South San Jose at 9.7 percent, and Santa Clara at 19.4 percent.

articles.title: Affordable housing plans emerge for San Jose site long in limbo with $15M land buy
articles.narrative: Green Valley Corporation and Pacific Housing have acquired a 13-acre site at 1655 Berryessa Road in San Jose for approximately $15 million, with plans to develop a 260-unit affordable housing project. The acquisition concludes decades of ownership by the Facchino family and is expected to deliver much-needed affordable housing near the Berryessa BART station. The project, named Berryessa Family Apartments, will offer 257 units at below-market rates for families earning 30 to 70 percent of the area median income.

data_points.notable_features: Located within walking distance to the Berryessa BART station; 257 units will be affordable, with three market-rate manager units.
data_points.project_notes: The site was previously owned by the Facchino family since 1972. A prior 2023 proposal for the site included up to 820 homes and 455,000 square feet of commercial space.

articles.title: Apartment Management Consultants Rolls Out Prisma Residential Portal Across Portfolio
articles.narrative: Apartment Management Consultants (AMC) has implemented Prisma Prop Tech's residential portal and payments platform across its extensive portfolio of over 900 apartment communities. This deployment aims to enhance the resident experience and optimize property performance through a unified digital platform offering rent payments, credit reporting, fraud protection, rewards programs, and maintenance features. The integration of Prisma's technology is a strategic move by AMC to leverage modern solutions for improved operational efficiency and property valuation.

articles.title: Alchemy Properties Plans Resi Conversion of Vacant Tribeca Garage
articles.narrative: Alchemy Properties, in partnership with Daishin America and Takamatsu Construction Group USA, has acquired a vacant five-story garage at 60 N. Moore St. in Tribeca for $57 million. The partnership plans a gut renovation and conversion of the 1914-vintage property into an ultra-luxury condominium, including the addition of two Landmarks-approved floors. This transaction highlights strong demand for high-end residential housing and growing investor confidence in the Manhattan market.

data_points.address: 60 N. Moore St., New York City, NY
data_points.notable_features: Landmarks-approved addition of two floors.
data_points.project_notes: Site currently hosts a vacant five-story car garage.

articles.title: Connect Media Expands Creative Agency Offering with Senior-Level Hires
Major League Soccer Unveils Global Headquarters at Vornado’s PENN 2
articles.narrative: Major League Soccer (MLS) has established its new global headquarters at Vornado Realty Trust's PENN 2 in Midtown Manhattan. The 126,000-square-foot, two-floor space is designed by TPG Architecture with an "office as a stadium" concept, featuring elements like a "player arrival" entry and a "Midfield" collaboration hub. JLL, which represented MLS in the long-term lease, noted that PENN 2's extensive amenities, its location within the evolving Penn District, and the lease's long-term flexibility made it an ideal choice for the league.

data_points.address: 2 Penn Plaza, New York City, NY
data_points.notable_features: Designed with an "office as a stadium" concept, including a "player arrival" entry and a "Midfield" collaboration hub. Located in a highly amenitized building within the Penn District.
data_points.project_notes: Long-term lease agreement.

articles.title: Insurance Drags Down Property Values By 17% In Climate-Sensitive Markets, Study Shows
articles.narrative: A new report by climate risk data firm First Street reveals that commercial real estate property values in climate-sensitive markets are 16.9% lower than in markets with lower climate risk. This valuation gap is primarily driven by the escalating and volatile costs of commercial property insurance, which saw a 154% increase between 2017 and 2024 across all major asset classes. Multifamily properties are particularly affected, experiencing a 207.5% rise in insurance costs per unit during the same period, leading to a 25% discount in trading values for high-risk multifamily assets. The study, based on 25 years of NCREIF data across 120 U.S. metro areas, highlights how increased insurance premiums are eroding net operating income, with insurance consuming 6.6% of multifamily NOI and 4.1% of all properties' NOI in 2024. While the reinsurance market is expected to soften, the underlying baseline for insurance rates continues to reset higher after each hardening period, indicating a persistent challenge for property owners in vulnerable areas.

companies_people.label: OTHER
companies_people.firm_name: National Council of Real Estate Investment Fiduciaries

articles.market_intelligence: Commercial real estate insurance costs grew by 154% between 2017 and 2024, with a compound annual growth rate of 14.3%. In contrast, the annual increase was 4.6% on average in the first 17 years of the 21st century. Multifamily insurance costs rose from $285.83 per unit in 2017 to $878.91 per unit in 2024, a 207.5% increase. Multifamily assets in high-risk markets have 69% higher premiums on average than those in low-risk areas, leading to a 25% discount in trading values. Insurance consumed 6.6% of multifamily NOI and 4.1% of all properties' NOI in 2024, up from 1.9% in 2017. Office values in Fort Lauderdale fell 17% between 2020 and 2024 as insurance costs doubled. Miami offices traded at an average of $360 per SF last year, nearly twice the national average of $190 per SF, and 38.5% above their valuations in Q1 2020.

articles.title: Ted Turner, Who Left A Mark On Downtown Atlanta CRE, Dies At 87
articles.narrative: Ted Turner, the founder of Turner Broadcasting, passed away at 87, leaving a significant mark on Downtown Atlanta's commercial real estate landscape. While not a developer, Turner's acquisition of a majority stake in the struggling 1.2M SF Omni International complex in 1985 from Cousins Properties transformed it into the iconic CNN Center, revitalizing the area. He later sold the property in 1988 for $170M, nearly tripling his initial investment. Turner also owned and resided in the historic Bona Allen Building, which housed Turner Enterprises and Ted's Montana Grill corporate offices, further cementing his presence in the city's core.

articles.title: Ideaya Biosciences Expanding Into 80K SF In South San Francisco
articles.narrative: Ideaya Biosciences has expanded its footprint at 5000 Shoreline Court in South San Francisco with a 25,000 SF lease expansion, bringing its total occupancy in the building to 80,000 SF. The clinical-stage cancer research company initially leased 44,000 SF in June 2023, followed by an 11,300 SF expansion in 2024, and now this latest addition. The property owner, DivcoWest, acquired the vacant 140,000 SF waterfront building in 2022 and has been converting it into laboratory space, investing significantly in tenant improvements and infrastructure upgrades.

data_points.address: 5000 Shoreline Court, South San Francisco, CA
data_points.notable_features: Waterfront building at Sierra Point, outfitted with a new 1,200-kilowatt generator system and upgraded electrical systems.
data_points.project_notes: DivcoWest previously acquired the vacant 140,000 SF building for $165M and converted it into laboratory space. Ideaya Biosciences' total occupancy in the building is now 80,000 SF, including prior expansions. DivcoWest will contribute $6.8M to tenant improvements for the current expansion.

articles.market_intelligence: In Q1, the average lease size in the Bay Area’s life sciences market was just under 20,000 SF, representing a 28.5% decline from the market’s five-year average lease size of nearly 28,000 SF.

articles.title: Pablo Castro Denies Laura Tauber Was Ever His Partner On 4,000-Unit HueHub Project
articles.narrative: A legal dispute has emerged over the HueHub project, a large-scale housing development in Miami. Developer Pablo Castro is refuting claims by Laura Tauber, who alleges she was a partner in the project, instrumental in securing approvals and union agreements, and is owed compensation and equity. Castro contends that Tauber was never a partner and her involvement was solely to learn about workforce housing development, with any future benefit contingent on her investment, which he claims never materialized. The project, which utilizes Florida's Live Local Act, has secured approval for its first phase, comprising over 4,000 units across six towers, with a significant portion dedicated to affordable housing for union members.

data_points.notable_features: Project utilizes Florida's Live Local Act, dedicating at least 40% of units to those making between 80% and 120% of the area median income. 70% of units are dedicated to members of police, firefighter, hospitality, teachers, and Miami-Dade County employee unions at an affordable rate for 10 years.
data_points.project_notes: Phase 1 approved for 4,032 units across six 35-story towers. The developer denies the project will be a $1.3B, 6,000-unit development at full build-out as claimed by the former partner. Final county approval for Phase 1 was granted in August, and a demolition ceremony was held on March 4.

articles.title: Commercial Property Prices Fall In April, Still Up 3.1% YOY
articles.narrative: Commercial property prices experienced a slight dip of 0.1% in April, marking the first decline of 2026, according to Green Street's Commercial Property Price Index. Despite this monthly decrease, overall commercial property values have shown a 3.1% increase over the past 12 months. The market is characterized by disciplined buyers, uninspiring rent growth in many sectors, and a 10-year Treasury yield in the mid-4s, which is dampening investor enthusiasm. The life sciences sector saw a 0.7% decline in April but remains up 5% year-over-year, trailing only data centers and strip retail, both up 6%.
articles.market_intelligence: The 10-year Treasury yield, a key determinant for CRE borrowing costs, has been in the mid-4s, influenced by the Iran conflict. The Federal Open Market Committee maintained its benchmark interest rate for the third consecutive meeting in April, with the Federal Reserve adopting a wait-and-see approach due to economic disruptions from immigration raids and changing tariff policies, exacerbated by oil price spikes.
